codeReview.summary: The feature adds the expected Room/DataStore/Keystore foundation, but I found two blocking correctness problems in the persistence layer and one non-blocking architecture-boundary drift in the domain model.

issues.description: `upsert()` persists the metadata row first and only then encrypts and writes the secret columns; `RoomSnippetRepository.upsert()` repeats the same pattern at `core/data/src/main/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/core/data/repository/RoomSnippetRepository.kt:24`. Because neither path uses a Room transaction, any failure between the first write and the second write can leave partially persisted foundation records (for example `hasSecret=true` with null/stale ciphertext, or a saved snippet metadata row with no encrypted body). For a security/data foundation layer, these writes need to be atomic.

The foundational schema does not declare foreign keys for cross-record references: `HostEntity.identityId`, `SessionMetadataEntity.hostId`, and `SnippetEntity.hostId` are plain columns, while `RoomIdentityRepository.deleteIdentity()` (`core/data/.../RoomIdentityRepository.kt:81`) and `RoomHostRepository.deleteHost()` (`core/data/.../RoomHostRepository.kt:37`) delete referenced rows unconditionally. That allows orphaned hosts, sessions, and targeted snippets, which conflicts with the documented host/identity ownership model and the invariant that snippet targets must not become stale or ambiguous.

The `Identity` model persists a `username`, but the architecture doc says hosts own username/endpoint metadata and identities own reusable secret/key material plus display metadata. Keeping username on identities duplicates connection metadata in the foundation layer and weakens the boundary later features are supposed to build on.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The android-feature-worker skill says workers should run the affected instrumentation/UI tests before handoff, but this worker added Android instrumentation suites and only reported `testDebugUnitTest` plus `lintDebug` reruns with no `connectedDebugAndroidTest` or manual checks. The procedure and/or validator expectations should be tightened so follow-up workers do not treat instrumentation coverage as optional when they add `androidTest` files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill: `.factory/skills/android-feature-worker/SKILL.md:24-25`. Handoff: `/root/.factory/missions/fc6a9d6d-b329-4b38-9f7c-ed2a18da2774/handoffs/2026-04-10T06-21-08-733Z__implement-local-data-and-security-foundation__764b6b1d-5dcf-4a27-838f-9024200906ac.json:16-38,51,56,81` shows only unit/lint commands, empty `interactiveChecks`, and newly added `core/data` / `core/security` androidTest files.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker reported that resolving secret-scanner false positives required non-functional formatting changes in addition to replacing synthetic fixture literals. That recovery path is not currently documented in the android-feature-worker skill, so future workers may spin unnecessarily on the same blocker.

summary: Fail: the foundation is mostly in place, but persistence writes are not atomic for encrypted records and the schema allows orphaned host/identity/session/snippet relationships. I also noted one non-blocking boundary drift (`Identity.username`) and two skill-documentation gaps.
